articles.title: Hackers Weaponize Balochistan Police Portal in Multi-Group Espionage Campaigns
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of sustained cyber espionage activity against several Pakistani law enforcement organizations undertaken by suspected China- and India-aligned threat actors between February 2024 and April 2026. "At Balochistan Police, the compromised assets included servers hosting web applications that manage police and citizen data, such as criminal and

articles.title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
articles.summary: Researchers at firmware security firm Binarly have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips inside data-center servers. Four of the bugs can crash a device. The other two could let an attacker who slips a malicious image in front of the bootloader run their own code, before the device

articles.title: Attacker Uses Suspected AI-Generated PowerShell Script to Map Active Directory
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an intrusion in which an unknown threat actor leveraged a vibe-coded PowerShell script for Active Directory (AD) enumeration. "The script looked for the Domain Controller (DC) and mapped users, computers, and domains, before creating a directory and exporting out a number of files, and finally creating AD_Report.html to measure the success of the

articles.title: Men disguised as police kill Ecuadorean drug lord's brother
articles.summary: The victim, David Macías, was the brother of drug lord 'El Fito', who is awaiting trial in the US.

articles.title: US Senator Mitch McConnell says absence due to fall and pneumonia
articles.summary: The veteran Republican says he will not return to work "quite yet", in his first statement following weeks of speculation about his health.

articles.title: The Download: a donor conception cap and world models for AI
articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. articles.title: Heavy rains and flash floods in Bangladesh leave 51 dead
articles.summary: Thousands have lost their homes as flash floods and landslides have hit large parts of the country.

articles.title: ‘We face a new ordeal’: Gaza's search for the dead goes on
articles.summary: After thousands of buildings were destroyed in the Gaza Strip during the Israel-Hamas war, local teams and civil defense personnel continue the arduous and delicate mission to remove rubble and search for the remains of missing persons believed to still be buried under the debris of homes destroyed by Israeli airstrikes.

articles.title: Forg365 PhaaS Targets Microsoft 365 with Device Code and AitM Session Theft
articles.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 is using a combination of device code phishing, adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) tactics, antibot evasion, artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted lure creation, and post-compromise mailbox operations targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. Distributed via Telegram and costing $400 a month (or $3,800 per year), attack chains leverage phishing

articles.title: iCagenda and Balbooa Forms Joomla Flaws Reportedly Exploited as Zero-Days
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added two maximum-severity security flaws impacting iCagenda and Balbooa extensions for Joomla to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of zero-day exploitation in the wild. The vulnerabilities, both rated 10.0 on the CVSS scoring system, are below - CVE-2026-48939 - A vulnerability in the

articles.title: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister
articles.summary: It was the first time firefighting planes had been sent up from the normally drier and hotter south of the country to tackle fires in the Paris region.

articles.title: German court convicts Iraqi couple of enslaving Yazidi girls
articles.summary: The pair, named only as Twana H.S. and Asia R. A., left Germany for Iraq in 2015 and became members of the Islamic State group.

articles.title: British couple return to village at heart of deadly Spanish wildfire
articles.summary: At least 13 people, including five believed to be Britons, were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Spain's Almeria province.

articles.title: From NASA to the classroom: The engineer bringing AI to those left behind
articles.summary: Nia Jetter spent two decades building spacecraft and robots. Now she is trying to make sure the artificial intelligence revolution doesn't leave anyone behind.

articles.title: UN chief urges Iran and US to ‘urgently resume negotiations’ as Gulf strikes escalate
articles.summary: Renewed strikes and counterstrikes between Iran and the United States in the Gulf region have raised fears of a return to all‑out war, with Washington denying Tehran’s claim that it had closed the crucial Strait of Hormuz on Sunday.

articles.title: Thai youth leaders push for inclusion, partnership and lasting change
articles.summary: From local communities to the global stage, a diverse group of young leaders from Thailand is helping shape conversations on public policy, climate action, inclusion, indigenous rights, disability access and youth wellbeing.

articles.title: Security Council LIVE: ‘Lost continuity of knowledge’ on Iran’s nuclear programme since US-Israel attacks, top UN official warns
articles.summary: Since the United States and Israel launched attacks on Iran in late February, there has been a “lost continuity of knowledge” on Tehran’s nuclear programme, Rosemary DiCarlo, the UN peace affairs chief, warned the Security Council on Friday as tensions mounted amid new strikes across the Middle East.

articles.title: Peak heat in Europe just broke historic 1970s records
articles.summary: This year marks the hottest June recorded for Western Europe and the second warmest globally, according to the latest report from a climate tracking service released on Thursday.

articles.title: Meta Files Patent for AI That Can Listen All Day and Track How You're Feeling
articles.summary: Meta has filed a patent application for an AI that listens to your voice throughout the day, works out how it thinks you are feeling from the way you sound, and keeps a timestamped log of every read. Each read gets pinned to the moment it happened: the time, your location, what you were doing, even how you were using your phone. Some versions in the filing would listen all day; others would

articles.title: Thinking Fast and Slow in the SOC: The Case for Combining Autonomous AI with Analyst Copilots
articles.summary: A few days ago, I was sitting with the CISO of a Fortune 50 company, walking through how his security team was thinking about AI agents in the SOC. Smart team. Serious program. They had already connected Claude to a few detection tools and were seeing real value in specific investigations. But as we mapped out the broader architecture, something kept nagging at me. The design they were building

articles.title: Misconfigured Server Reveals Three Evilginx Phishing Operations Targeting Microsoft 365
articles.summary: An attacker running a live Microsoft 365 phishing operation left a Python web server listening on a public port with directory listing switched on. The command that did it: python3 -m http.server 8080, was still sitting in the readable .bash_history. From that one lapse, French security firm Lexfo lifted the operator's entire toolkit and pivoted through it to two more

articles.title: Critical Zimbra Flaw Could Let Crafted Emails Run Malicious Code in User Sessions
articles.summary: Zimbra is urging customers to apply updates to address a critical security vulnerability impacting the Classic Web Client that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability has been described as a case of stored cross-site scripting (XSS) that could allow specially crafted emails to execute malicious scripts in a user's session. It has yet to be assigned a CVE identifier. "The

articles.title: URGENT - Progress Tells ShareFile Customers to Shut Down Storage Zone Controllers Over Security Threat
articles.summary: Progress Software has told ShareFile customers to shut down the Windows servers running their Storage Zone Controllers, confirming to The Hacker News that it is responding to a "credible external security threat." The company has temporarily disabled access to the affected accounts, a step it says it took "out of an abundance of caution" while it works with internal and external security

articles.summary: As governments turn to the ocean for jobs, growth and climate solutions, campaigners at the Our Ocean Conference in Mombasa warned coastal communities must not be left behind The post As blue economy gathers pace, communities must benefit from ocean boom, activists say appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Senator Lindsey Graham died of aortic tear, examiner says
articles.summary: Graham, a close Republican ally of Donald Trump, died at the age of 71 on Saturday evening.

articles.title: Infantino opens door to 64-team World Cup
articles.summary: Plans for a 64-team men's world cup are set to be picked up and assessed in detail after the 2026 tournament with Fifa boss Gianni Infantino saying football needs to be "for the whole world".

articles.title: South Africa says more than 53,000 foreigners deported in migration campaign
articles.summary: South Africa is cracking down on undocumented migrants following widespread anti-immigration protests.

articles.title: China evacuates nearly two million people as powerful typhoon makes landfall
articles.summary: Schools, work and outdoor activities have been suspended in Zhejiang province and many transport services cancelled.

articles.title: Reviving communities home by home in Gaza
articles.summary: Displacement has drastically reshaped every aspect of daily life in war-ravaged Gaza for 24-year-old Mayyada, who is seven months pregnant with twins and caring for her two-year-old daughter, but now she and her family are getting settled into a temporary home of their own.

articles.title: New cholera outbreak alert for Sudan’s war-weary communities
articles.summary: In war-torn Sudan, a deadly new cholera outbreak has already claimed more than 100 lives, heightening serious concerns for vulnerable communities including in besieged El-Obeid, where daily drone attacks have continued to hamper aid access.

articles.title: At least 28 killed, 25 critically injured after fire engulfs Bangkok bar
articles.summary: Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through flames to escape.

articles.title: 'Hero, legend, sweetheart': Tributes to Jurassic Park actor Sam Neill, who has died aged 78
articles.summary: Friends, politicians and fellow film stars remember Neill following his "sudden and unexpected" death.

articles.title: T. rex could become most expensive fossil ever - but it's a ​problem for scientists
articles.summary: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.

articles.title: Heatwave to stretch into second week despite cooler air for some this weekend
articles.summary: The sizzling heat continues into next week despite a drop in temperatures over the weekend.

articles.title: New dinosaur species as long as cricket pitch discovered in Thailand
articles.summary: The plant-eating dinosaur, named Uragasaurus kalasinensis, is thought to have lived about 150 million years ago.

articles.title: Boxy forms and odd lights: US declassifies more UFO files
articles.summary: The US government published a fourth instalment of unresolved cases, promising to release more files on a rolling basis.

articles.summary: See how the powerful storm is expected to move across east Asia on Saturday.

articles.title: South Sudan: UN acts ahead of drought to protect thousands
articles.summary: As drought risk intensifies in South Sudan’s Eastern Equatoria state, a new UN-backed plan aims to protect thousands of people before the worst impacts take hold.

articles.title: Wild, Scenic, and Increasingly Rusty
articles.summary: Orange streams are now being spotted in hundreds of watersheds in permafrost areas throughout Alaska’s Brooks Range.
